name: New York
tooltip: New York — Cat 1: Strong digital replica statute, just amended December 2025. Domicile-based, so works for NY-based performers.
rightOfPublicity: Yes — N.Y. Civ. Rights Law §§ 50, 51 (the original 1903 statute, still in force) protects living persons against unauthorized commercial use of name, portrait, picture, or voice. Statutory minimum damages of $750 plus actual damages, profits, and attorney's fees.
postMortem: Yes — 40 years after death for "deceased personalities" (people whose name, likeness, etc. had commercial value at the time of death) domiciled in New York. N.Y. Civ. Rights Law § 50-f.
aiCoverage: Yes — and recently strengthened. § 50-f covers digital replicas in scripted audiovisual works. § 52-c creates a private right of action for sexually explicit deepfakes. Most importantly, S.8391/A.8882, Chapter 616 of the Laws of 2025, signed by Governor Hochul on December 11, 2025 (effective immediately), broadened § 50-f to track California's AB 1836/AB 2602 language: now covers any audiovisual work, sound recording, or live performance — not just "scripted" content — and removes the disclaimer-as-safe-harbor that had been a loophole.
remedy: Civil action under § 50, § 51, § 50-f, or § 52-c. $750 statutory floor plus damages, profits, and fees. Critical procedural note: New York applies its post-mortem right based on the deceased's domicile at death, so estates of NY-domiciled performers can use § 50-f wherever the violation occurred.
whatsInteresting: New York's December 11, 2025 amendment was a quiet but major upgrade. Pre-amendment, § 50-f only covered "scripted audiovisual works" — meaning if someone made a deepfake of you in a music video or live performance, the statute didn't reach it. Post-amendment, all audiovisual work, all sound recordings, and live performances are in. The law was signed at SAG-AFTRA's New York Local with union backing, which tells you who pushed for it. If you're a performer who works in New York and your likeness shows up in something AI-generated, this is the statute that just got a lot stronger. Chapter 616 confirmed per official NYS legislative action records; running § 50-f text and bound Laws of New York volume to be spot-checked once published.
primarySources: S.8391 / A.8882, Chapter 616 of the Laws of 2025 (signed Dec. 11, 2025, effective immediately) — [nysenate.gov](http://nysenate.gov)
S.8420-A / A.8887-B, Chapter 617 of the Laws of 2025 (Gen. Bus. Law § 396-b, synthetic-performer advertising disclosure, effective June 9, 2026)
